description: Vercel atomic-immutable edge deploy. DECLARATIVE STUB — no runtime client shipped. Fields derived from official Vercel documentation accessed 2026-04-30: /docs/instant-rollback, /docs/deployments/rollback-production-deployment, /docs/cli/rollback, /docs/cli/inspect, /docs/environment-variables. Refine when first Vercel client is implemented.

required_env_vars.name: VERCEL_TOKEN
required_env_vars.description: Vercel API token for CLI authentication.

optional_env_vars.name: VERCEL_ORG_ID
optional_env_vars.description: Organization/team ID. Optional if the project is linked locally via `vercel link`.

optional_env_vars.name: VERCEL_PROJECT_ID
optional_env_vars.description: Project ID. Optional if the project is linked locally.

rollback.manual_procedure_reference: vercel rollback [deployment-url] (or: vercel promote [deployment-url] to undo a rollback). Status: vercel rollback status. Inspect provenance: vercel inspect [deployment-url].

known_gaps: Custom aliases are NOT auto-rolled-back during instant-rollback; they remain pointed at the previously-aliased deployment unless manually re-aliased.
Vercel has no built-in health-check rollback — auto_trigger is honestly 'off'. Auto-rollback on failure must be implemented by external CI/CD logic.
Hobby plan is limited to rolling back to the immediately previous production deployment. Pro and Enterprise plans can roll back to any eligible (previously production-aliased) deployment.

Preview deployments are not modeled in this v1.0 schema scope (which fixes 'dev' + 'prod'). Vercel's preview-per-branch concept could be added under environments.{branch-name} in v2.0 via the existing patternProperties.

Environment variables are baked into the deployment at build time — rolling back to an old deployment ALSO rolls back to its env-var snapshot. Profile does not encode this nuance; covered in plugin-side discipline doc.
